title: Built Heritage Committee - April 14, 2026
meeting_location: Champlain Room, 110 Laurier Avenue West, and by electronic participation

agenda_items.title: Notices and meeting information for meeting participants and the public

attachments.attachment_title: Public Notices English - Hybrid Standing Committee 2025 (AODA).pdf
Public Notices French - Hybrid Standing Committee 2025 (AODA).pdf

agenda_items.description:
Notices and meeting information are attached to the agenda and minutes, including: availability of simultaneous interpretation and accessibility accommodations; in camera meeting procedures; information items not subject to discussion; personal information disclaimer for correspondents and public speakers; notices regarding minutes; and hybrid participation details.
Accessible formats and communication supports are available, upon request.
Except where otherwise indicated, reports requiring Council consideration will be presented to Council on Wednesday, 22 April 2026 in Built Heritage Committee Report 31.
The deadline to register by phone to speak, or submit written comments or visual presentations is 4 pm on Monday, 13 April 2026, and the deadline to register by email to speak is 8:30 am on Tuesday, 14 April 2026.
agenda_items.minutes: These “Summary Minutes” indicate the disposition of items and actions taken at the meeting. This document does not include all of the text that will be included in the full Minutes, such as the record of written and oral submissions. Recorded votes and dissents contained in the Summary Minutes are draft until the full Minutes of the meeting are confirmed by the Committee. The draft of the full Minutes (for confirmation) will be published with the agenda for the next regular Committee meeting and, once confirmed, will replace this document.

agenda_items.title: Declarations of Interest
agenda_items.minutes: No Declarations of Interest were filed.

agenda_items.title: Planning, Development and Building Services Department

sub_agendas_items.title: Heritage Permit – 267 O’Connor Street
sub_agendas_items.minutes:
This item will also be considered by the Planning and Housing Committee at its meeting of Wednesday, 15 April 2026.
At the outset, the Committee received a detailed slide presentation overview of the report from Anne Fitzpatrick, Planner III, Heritage Planning, Planning, Development and Building Services (PDBS) Department.  A copy of this presentation is held on file with the Office of the City Clerk.  
The Committee then heard from the following delegations:
Correspondence was also received from the following and is also held on file with the office of the City Clerk:
Following questions to the delegations, Councillor Leiper put forth the following Motion:
Motion NO BHC 2026 31-01
Moved by Councillor J. Leiper:
WHEREAS the report recommendation that approval be conditional upon “the applicant working with Heritage Planning staff to explore the feasibility of conserving the relief sculpture on the upper storey of the existing building,” and;
WHEREAS the relief sculpture is worthy of conservation as a heritage and cultural asset;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED that the recommended condition c. be amended to read: “The applicant conserving the relief sculpture in a manner satisfactory to Heritage Planning staff.”
Following questions to staff, and with Committee discussions also having been concluded, Motion NO 2026 31-01 was put before Committee and was ‘Carried’.  
The report recommendations, as amended by Motion NO 2026 31-01 were then put before Committee, and were ‘Lost’ on a division of four ‘Yeas’ to four ‘Nays’, subsequent to which Member Legault requested that her original ‘Nay’ vote be amended to a ‘Yea’.  With the will of Committee, Member Legault’s vote was amended, and the report recommendations, as amended by Motion NO 2026 31-01 were thus ‘Carried’, on a division of five ‘Yeas’ to three ‘Nays, as follows:
For (5):    Members B. Bourns and C. Legault, and Councillors C. Kelly, J. Leiper and Chair R. King
Against (3):    Member C. Quinn, Councillor A. Troster and Vice-Chair S. Plante
Report recommendation(s) as amended
That the Built Heritage Committee and Planning and Housing Committee recommend that Council:
Carried, as amended

sub_agendas_items.title: Ontario Heritage Act Changes - Implementation Update
sub_agendas_items.minutes:
The Committee received a detailed slide presentation from Lesley Collins, Program Manager, Heritage Planning, Planning, Development and Building Services (PDBS) Department, which served as an overview of the report.  A copy of this presentation is held on file with the Office of the City Clerk.
Prior to hearing from the public delegations, the Vice-Chair read the following Motions aloud:
Motion NO BHC 2026 31-02
Moved by Member B. Bourns:
WHEREAS the City’s Official Plan enables the designation of groups of buildings and areas of the city as Heritage Conservation Districts under Part V of the Ontario Heritage Act; and WHEREAS Heritage Conservation Districts should include significant groupings of historic buildings;THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT the Built Heritage Committee recommend Council approve that staff be directed to consider the following when establishing HCD boundaries:
Motion NO BHC 2026 31-03
Moved by Councillor R. King:
WHEREAS the City of Ottawa has adopted a new Comprehensive Zoning By-law (2026-50), which will guide land use, built form, and development across the city; and
WHEREAS Heritage Conservation Districts (HCDs) are governed by approved HCD Plans under Part V of the Ontario Heritage Act, which provide direction on heritage-sensitive form and character; and
WHEREAS residents and stakeholders have raised questions regarding how HCD Plans will continue to apply and interact with the new Zoning By-law, particularly following the removal of the former heritage overlay; and
WHEREAS clear, accessible, and consistent information is essential to support transparency, public understanding, and confidence in the development review process within HCDs;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED that the Built Heritage Committee recommend Council approve that staff be directed to prepare and publish a “Heritage and the New Zoning By-law” Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) resource; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the FAQ include, at minimum:
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that staff circulate a copy of the final document to the Built Heritage Committee as part of the implementation of the new Zoning By-law.
The Committee then heard from the following delegations:
Correspondence was also received from the following and is also held on file with the office of the City Clerk:
Following questions to the delegations and to staff, and with Committee discussions also having concluded, Motion NO BHC 2026 31-02 was put before Committee and was ‘Lost’ on a division of one ‘Yea’ to seven ‘Nays’ as outlined below.
For (1):    Member B. Bourns
Against (7):    Members C. Quinn and C. Legault and Councillors C. Kelly, J. Leiper, A. Troster, Vice-Chair S. Plante and Chair R. King
Lost (1 to 7)
Motion NO BHC 2026 31-03 was then put before Committee and was ‘Carried’ as presented.  The report recommendations were then put before Committee and were ‘Carried’, as amended by Motion No. BHC 2026 31-03.

motions.motion_text:
Report Recommendation(s)
That the Built Heritage Committee recommend that Council:

WHEREAS the City of Ottawa has adopted a new Comprehensive Zoning By-law (2026-50), which will guide land use, built form, and development across the city; and
WHEREAS Heritage Conservation Districts (HCDs) are governed by approved HCD Plans under Part V of the Ontario Heritage Act, which provide direction on heritage-sensitive form and character; and
WHEREAS residents and stakeholders have raised questions regarding how HCD Plans will continue to apply and interact with the new Zoning By-law, particularly following the removal of the former heritage overlay; and
WHEREAS clear, accessible, and consistent information is essential to support transparency, public understanding, and confidence in the development review process within HCDs;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED that the Built Heritage Committee recommend Council approve that staff be directed to prepare and publish a “Heritage and the New Zoning By-law” Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) resource; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the FAQ include, at minimum:
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that staff circulate a copy of the final document to the Built Heritage Committee as part of the implementation of the new Zoning By-law.
